Discovery - Motion To Compel Further Responses
Off Calendar Nov-17-2016 Continued To Dec-09-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

Counsel for plaintiff/opposing party, having requested a continuance due to a medical emergency, DEFENDANT JONATHAN MANIERI and MICHAEL MANIERI's Motion To Compel Further Responses is CONTINUED to December 9, 2016 at 9:00 AM in Department 302 on the Court's own motion.

Judge Pro Tem: David Helbraun; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Not Reported. (D302/jpt)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-14-2016 Continued To Dec-14-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Sep-14-2016 Continued To Nov-02-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Motion And Notice Of Hearing For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Sep-21-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Preetpaul S. Khangura of Santana Vierra & Symonds (415-777-1308) specially appears as counsel for defendant/moving party, Michael Manieri

No other appearance is made in this matter.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT MICHAEL MANIERI'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings is GRANTED with twenty days leave to amend. The operative complaint, the first amended complaint, does not allege that plaintiff was ignorant of his claims against Mr. Manieri when the initial complaint was filed. Absent such an allegation, the first amended complaint shows that the claims against Mr. Manieri are time-barred. Plaintiff is granted leave to file a second amended complaint seeking to invoke CCP 474 if he can do so in good faith. If he does so, Mr. Manieri's remedy, if any, would be to challenge the applicability of CCP 474 on an evidentiary motion such as a motion for summary judgment or at trial. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Not Reported. (D302)
